title: Signup failed because audit_log table was missing in production
root_cause: Production database schema was behind application code. The backend attempted to insert into audit_log during default location creation, but the audit_log table did not exist.
fix: Run Alembic migrations through head before promoting the backend deployment.

preconditions: Deploy backend code with audit logging enabled.
Do not apply the Alembic revision that creates audit_log.
steps: Submit POST /auth/signup with tenant_name, email, and password.
Observe a 500 response and a backend traceback mentioning relation audit_log does not exist.
reproduction.expected: Signup succeeds and creates tenant, user, and default location.
reproduction.actual: Commit fails at session.commit because audit_log is missing.

title: Appointment lifecycle flow must remain reproducible under simulation
symptom: Operational mutations can drift from expected behavior if not exercised repeatedly.
root_cause: Lifecycle paths such as reschedule, cancel, no_show, and complete are easy to break when the product evolves.
fix: Keep a deterministic operational regression test and a reproducible simulator with persisted manifests.

preconditions: Have a tenant with at least one user, location, customer, and service.
steps: Mutate some to completed, some to cancelled, some to no_show, and reschedule others.
Verify list and analytics outputs remain coherent.
reproduction.expected: Lifecycle mutations succeed and are queryable afterwards.
reproduction.actual: Any future drift should be caught by the automated regression suite.
